games.title: Wordle
games.description: The original 5-letter word guessing game. A daily ritual for millions — six attempts, one word. Simple, satisfying, and perfect for the two minutes between calls.

games.title: Connections
games.description: Group 16 words into four categories — the tricky part is the categories are never what they seem. Tests lateral thinking as much as vocabulary. Addictive once you start.
games.curator_note: Tests the kind of pattern recognition useful in any pitch.

games.title: NYT Mini Crossword
games.description: A 5×5 crossword that takes 60–90 seconds when you're on form. Surprisingly good for warming up your brain before a site visit or client presentation.

games.title: GeoGuessr
games.description: Drop into a random Google Street View location and guess where in the world you are. Geography fluency matters in a global industry — and the architectural cues are genuinely revealing.
games.curator_note: You will start noticing building materials by continent.

games.title: Worldle
games.description: Guess the country from its silhouette. Each wrong answer gives you the distance and direction to the correct one. Sharpens the geographic awareness every international fit-out professional should have.

games.title: Flagle
games.description: Guess the country from progressively revealed segments of its flag. Six guesses, one flag. Useful for anyone pitching or delivering projects across multiple jurisdictions.
games.curator_note: Know your client's flag before you know their brief.

games.title: Seterra — World Capitals Quiz
games.description: Click the correct capital city on a world map. A browser-based geography drill that's genuinely useful if your pipeline spans multiple continents. Free, no login required.
games.curator_note: For project teams who need to know their markets cold.

games.title: Sporcle — Iconic Buildings Quiz
games.description: Name the world's most recognisable buildings against the clock. A useful shorthand check on architectural literacy — and a good team game before a global client visit.

games.title: Nerdle
games.description: Wordle for numbers and equations. Guess the hidden maths equation in six tries. Recommended for estimators, QSs, and anyone who thinks in costs per square metre.
games.curator_note: For the QS who needs a daily number fix.

games.title: Redactle Unlimited
games.description: Guess the Wikipedia article with all meaningful words redacted. Reveal words by guessing them one at a time. Tests deductive reasoning under ambiguity — exactly the skill needed in early project scoping.

games.title: Skyscraper Puzzle
games.description: A spatial logic puzzle: fill a grid with buildings of different heights so that each row and column shows the correct number of visible skyscrapers. Demands the same spatial reasoning as reading a fit-out plan.
games.curator_note: Spatial logic — the skill that separates good from great site managers.

games.title: Human Resource Machine
games.description: Program a little office worker to move items between bins using simple logic instructions. A puzzle game that teaches computational thinking through the lens of an absurd workplace — oddly relatable.
games.curator_note: Workplace logic puzzles that feel uncomfortably real.

games.title: Material & Finish Quiz (Architextures)
games.description: Browse and test knowledge of construction materials, textures, and finishes. A specifier's reference that doubles as an education tool. Explore material categories used globally in commercial interior construction.
games.curator_note: Know your finishes as well as your client does.

games.title: Quordle
games.description: Solve four Wordle puzzles simultaneously with the same nine guesses. Each letter you play reveals information across all four boards. Requires managing multiple parallel workstreams — not unlike running a fit-out programme.

games.title: Immaculate Grid (Architecture Edition via Trivia)
games.description: NYT Strands: find themed word groups hidden in a 6×8 letter grid. One bonus word links all the themes together. Meditative, clever, and genuinely satisfying to complete.
games.curator_note: Theme-spotting across the grid. Like reading a complex brief.
